Neural Pfaffian (NeurPf) is proposed as a method that utilizes Pfaffians to define a superset of previously used Slater determinants to enforce fermionic antisymmetry. It is compared to Globe and TAO in terms of modeling non-equilibrium, ionized, or excited systems. NeurPf achieves significantly lower variational energies and demonstrates superior accuracy compared to Globe.

Our Neural Pfafﬁan is more accurate, simpler to implement, fully learnable, and applicable to any molecular system compared to previous work.

Additionally, we introduced a memory-efﬁcient implementation of the exponential envelopes, reducing memory requirements while accelerating convergence. Further, we presented a pretraining scheme for Pfafﬁans enabling initialization with Hartree-Fock – a crucial step for molecular systems.

Our experimental evaluation demonstrated that our Neural Pfafﬁan can generalize across different ionizations of various systems, stay accurate when enlarging datasets, and set a new state of the art by outperforming previous neural wave functions and the reference CCSD(T) CBS on the TinyMol dataset.
